skill.name: vercel-react-best-practices
skill.description: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering. This skill should be used when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code to ensure optimal performance patterns. Triggers on tasks involving React components, Next.js pages, data fetching, bundle optimization, or performance improvements.
skill.summary: Comprehensive React and Next.js performance optimization guide with 45 rules across 8 priority categories for automated refactoring and code generation.
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: All 911 static findings are false positives. This is a documentation skill containing React/Next.js best practices with code examples. The flagged patterns (backticks, storage access, dynamic imports) are legitimate documentation content, not security risks.

content.value_statement: This skill provides 45 performance optimization rules for React and Next.js applications, helping developers eliminate waterfalls, reduce bundle sizes, and improve rendering performance through automated guidance.
actual_capabilities: Apply 45 performance rules across 8 priority categories
Eliminate async waterfalls with Promise.all and Suspense
Optimize bundle size with dynamic imports and code splitting
Reduce re-renders with memo, useCallback, and proper dependencies
Improve server-side performance with caching and parallel fetching
limitations: Does not execute code or make changes to files directly
Requires manual implementation of suggested patterns
Does not cover all React libraries or frameworks beyond Next.js

use_cases.title: Generate Optimized React Components
use_cases.description: When writing new React components, the skill guides you to use memo, proper dependencies, and avoid common pitfalls like inline functions in JSX.

use_cases.title: Refactor Slow Next.js Pages
use_cases.description: When optimizing existing Next.js pages, the skill helps identify waterfalls, parallelize data fetching, and implement proper caching strategies.

use_cases.title: Code Review for Performance
use_cases.description: When reviewing pull requests, the skill provides a checklist of 45 rules to ensure code meets performance standards before merging.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Performance Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React component for performance issues and suggest improvements based on the vercel-react-best-practices skill rules.

prompt_templates.title: Next.js Page Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Optimize this Next.js page for faster load times. Identify any async waterfalls and suggest parallel data fetching patterns.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - optimizing a page with multiple data dependencies

prompt_templates.title: Bundle Analysis and Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this codebase for bundle size issues. Identify large dependencies that should be dynamically imported and suggest code splitting strategies.

prompt_templates.title: Complete Performance Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a complete performance audit of this Next.js application using all 45 rules from vercel-react-best-practices. Prioritize by impact category.

output_examples.input:
Review this component for performance: function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return
setCount(count + 1)}>{count}
; }
output_examples.output: Issues found: 1) Inline function in onClick creates new function on every render - use useCallback. 2) No memoization - wrap component with React.memo if parent re-renders frequently. Rule: rerender-functional-setstate, rerender-memo

output_examples.input: Optimize this data fetching: const user = await getUser(id); const posts = await getPosts(user.id); const comments = await getComments(user.id);
output_examples.output: Waterfall detected - posts and comments both depend on user but fetch sequentially. Fix: Use Promise.all([getPosts(user.id), getComments(user.id)]). Rule: async-parallel

best_practices: Always use Promise.all for independent async operations to eliminate waterfalls
Use React.memo and useCallback only when needed - premature optimization adds complexity
Implement server-side caching with React.cache() for repeated function calls
anti_patterns: Avoid inline functions in JSX props - they create new references on every render
Do not fetch data inside components when it can be done at build time or server side
Avoid subscribing to expensive objects in useEffect dependencies

faq.question: What makes this skill different from general React tutorials?
faq.answer: This skill contains 45 specific, actionable rules prioritized by Vercel Engineering based on real-world performance impact.

faq.question: Do I need to implement all 45 rules?
faq.answer: No. Rules are organized by priority. Start with CRITICAL (waterfalls, bundle size) before moving to MEDIUM and LOW impact rules.

faq.question: Can this skill automatically fix performance issues?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides guidance and recommendations. You must manually implement the suggested patterns in your code.

faq.question: Does this work with Create React App or only Next.js?
faq.answer: Most rules apply to any React application. Server-side rules like server-cache-react are Next.js specific.

faq.question: How do I know which rule to apply first?
faq.answer: Rules are prefixed by priority: async-* (CRITICAL), bundle-* (CRITICAL), server-* (HIGH), client-* (MEDIUM-HIGH), rerender-* (MEDIUM).

faq.question: Can I use this for code review automation?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill provides a comprehensive checklist that can guide manual code review or be used to evaluate generated code.
